description: Presentation development workflow steps extracted from course lessons for kanban board population

phases.name: The Start
phases.description: Define your audience and presentation goals

courseProjectTasks.task: Stay out of PowerPoint!
courseProjectTasks.description: Resist the urge to open PowerPoint too early in the process

courseProjectTasks.task: Select a presentation that you would like to create as your course project
courseProjectTasks.description: Choose a real presentation to work on throughout the course

courseProjectTasks.task: Identify the audience for your presentation
courseProjectTasks.description: Determine who will be receiving your presentation

courseProjectTasks.task: Create an audience map for your audience
courseProjectTasks.description: Document key characteristics, needs, and expectations of your audience

courseProjectTasks.task: Identify what your audience already knows; build the context portion of your introduction
courseProjectTasks.description: Establish common ground with your audience

courseProjectTasks.task: Determine the catalyst for the presentation
courseProjectTasks.description: What has happened that has created the need for this presentation?

courseProjectTasks.task: Determine what the question is that you are answering
courseProjectTasks.description: Clarify the central question your presentation addresses

courseProjectTasks.task: Write down a single goal or next step for your presentation
courseProjectTasks.description: Define the ultimate objective of your presentation

courseProjectTasks.task: Think about the component pieces of your goal
courseProjectTasks.description: Start thinking about what an Action Agenda for the first 72 hours might look like

courseProjectTasks.task: Gather as much useful content as possible to answer your question
courseProjectTasks.description: Collect existing content and new analysis

courseProjectTasks.task: Run a brainstorming session to generate new ideas and approaches
courseProjectTasks.description: Write everything down when answering your question

courseProjectTasks.task: Select a visual thinking tool to use to keep track of your ideas
courseProjectTasks.description: Choose a method to organize and visualize your thoughts

courseProjectTasks.task: Create an Argument Map for your answer to the question
courseProjectTasks.description: Visually map out your reasoning structure

courseProjectTasks.task: Start by building an inductive argument that supports your answer
courseProjectTasks.description: Build logical support for your conclusion

courseProjectTasks.task: Structure your supporting evidence and ideas into groups of similar ideas
courseProjectTasks.description: Ensure your groups are MECE and contain no more than 4-5 key ideas

courseProjectTasks.task: Identify how and where you might be able to inject a story into your presentation
courseProjectTasks.description: Find opportunities for narrative elements

courseProjectTasks.task: Consider adding a story at the beginning, middle, or end of the presentation
courseProjectTasks.description: Determine optimal placement for maximum impact

courseProjectTasks.task: Design your story to be a story 'that sticks'
courseProjectTasks.description: Make your story memorable and impactful

courseProjectTasks.task: Start thinking about how you might apply these techniques to your own presentation
courseProjectTasks.description: Consider film storyboarding principles

courseProjectTasks.task: Complete the next lesson before you start creating your own storyboard
courseProjectTasks.description: Prepare for the practical storyboarding exercise

courseProjectTasks.task: Create your storyboard; write it out on paper
courseProjectTasks.description: Draft your presentation flow on paper first

courseProjectTasks.task: Iterate the storyboard several times
courseProjectTasks.description: Focus first on key messages, then on flow, and finally on supporting arguments

phases.name: Design
phases.description: Apply visual design principles and create slides

courseProjectTasks.task: Continue to refine your storyboard with emphasis on visual communication
courseProjectTasks.description: Strike the balance between text and visual forms (charts, graphs, diagrams, frameworks)

lessons.title: Overview of the Fundamental Elements of Design
The Fundamental Elements of Design in Detail

courseProjectTasks.task: Use Adobe Color Wheel to create a color palette for your presentation
courseProjectTasks.description: Define your presentation's color scheme

courseProjectTasks.task: Select a set of custom fonts to use for your presentation
courseProjectTasks.description: Download and install them in PowerPoint

courseProjectTasks.task: Review your Storyboard for instances where you are breaking design principles
courseProjectTasks.description: Pay particular attention to whitespace, alignment, and proximity

courseProjectTasks.task: Apply Gestalt principles to your slide designs
courseProjectTasks.description: Use proximity, similarity, and other Gestalt principles effectively

courseProjectTasks.task: Move beyond storyboarding and start to design your slides in detail
courseProjectTasks.description: Transition from planning to actual slide creation

courseProjectTasks.task: Decide which data in your presentation should be tables vs graphs
courseProjectTasks.description: Choose the right format for each data point

courseProjectTasks.task: Create basic charts for your presentation data
courseProjectTasks.description: Apply best practices for bar charts, line charts, and pie charts

courseProjectTasks.task: Consider if specialized chart types would better communicate your data
courseProjectTasks.description: Explore waterfall charts, scatter plots, and other advanced visualizations

courseProjectTasks.task: Write your voiceover script
courseProjectTasks.description: Document what you will say for each slide

courseProjectTasks.task: Memorize your script
courseProjectTasks.description: Know your content well enough to deliver naturally

courseProjectTasks.task: Focus delivery efforts on the 4 key areas discussed in the lesson
courseProjectTasks.description: Apply the core delivery techniques

courseProjectTasks.task: Test the length of your presentation and edit to fit allotted time
courseProjectTasks.description: Ensure your presentation fits the time constraints

courseProjectTasks.task: Practice in front of others and solicit feedback
courseProjectTasks.description: Get external input on your delivery

courseProjectTasks.task: Develop a shorter 'Plan B' deck
courseProjectTasks.description: Prepare a condensed version for time-constrained situations

courseProjectTasks.task: Record video of yourself presenting and watch it
courseProjectTasks.description: Self-review your delivery for improvement opportunities

kanbanMapping.description: Suggested mapping of tasks to kanban columns

priorities.high: Critical path tasks that block other work
